onboarding.title: PostHog Integration
onboarding.subtitle: Event capture, feature flags, and event queries
onboarding.sampleDataDescription: No sample data — this is a service template that calls the PostHog API.

steps.title: Configure API Key
steps.description: Enter your PostHog Project API key. For self-hosted or EU instances, also set the instance URL.

steps.title: Capture Events & Evaluate Flags
steps.description: Use the project key to capture events and evaluate feature flags from the desktop app or via AI.

steps.title: Optional: Query Events
steps.description: To list past events, also configure a Personal API Key and Project ID — required by PostHog's events query endpoint.

reference.overview: PostHog is a product analytics platform. This service template wraps the most common ingest and query endpoints.

concepts.name: Event Capture
concepts.description: Track custom events for users with arbitrary properties via /capture/.

concepts.name: Feature Flags
concepts.description: Evaluate feature flags for a user via /decide/?v=3 — including multivariate variants and payloads.

concepts.name: Event Queries
concepts.description: List recent events from your project. Requires a Personal API Key (Bearer auth) and the project ID.

prompts: Track an event 'signup_completed' for user user_42 with property plan='pro'
What feature flags does user_42 have enabled?
tips: Use a stable distinct_id per user so events join correctly across sessions
Feature flag changes can take a minute or two to propagate to /decide/
list-events needs a Personal API Key — the Project API Key isn't authorized for it
For EU customers set POSTHOG_INSTANCE to https://eu.posthog.com
